DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS TWITCH INTERACTIVE, INC., AMAZON.COM, INC., AMAZON ADVERTISING, LLC, AND ADAM HARRIS'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled in part and sustained in part.

The demurrer has three grounds.

First, Adam Harris alone demurs to the second cause of action for "hostile work environment harassment in violation of the FEHA." While not in the high range of hostility or harassment, the FAC's allegations against Harris are adequate for the pleading stage. (Id. at 1:24-27, 7:11-10:27, 21:16-22.) This part of the demurrer is overruled.

Second, Twitch Interactive, Inc., Amazon.com, Inc. and Amazon Advertising, LLC demur to the fifth and sixth causes of action for "breach of express oral contract not to terminate without good cause" and "breach of implied-in-fact contract not to terminate employment without good cause." The FAC's contract allegations are based on plaintiff's promotions, but no authority is cited for a notion that promotions alone can form an express oral, or implied-in-fact, contract not to terminate without good cause. (Id. at 4:16-21, 9:24-27; Opp. 6:16-20, 7:9-12.) This part of the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Third, Harris demurs to the tenth cause of action for "intentional infliction of emotional distress." Plaintiff agrees that the FAC must plead "extreme and outrageous" conduct by Harris. It does not do so; not all discrimination allegations suffice. (Ankeny v. Lockheed Missiles & Space Co. (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 531.) This part of the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
